Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family, COMT subfamily
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. COMT subfamily. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=(E)-anol + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = (E)-anethole + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:36247, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:35616, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:73343; EC=2.1.1.279; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:18987218}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=(E)-isoeugenol + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = (E)-isomethyleugenol + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:17081, ChEBI:CHEBI:6877, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:50545, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789; EC=2.1.1.279; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:18987218};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Phenylpropene O-methyltransferase that catalyzes the conversion of trans-anol to trans-anethole and isoeugenol to isomethyleugenol. Phenylpropenes are the primary constituents of various essential plant oils. They are produced as antimicrobial and antianimal compounds, or as floral attractants of pollinators. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:18987218}.
